adds.rationale: Eli Lilly's Mounjaro (tirzepatide) is the cleanest pure-play on the aging-population/obesity demographics wave; Q1 2023 sales of ~$570M plus the SURMOUNT-2 readout position it for an obesity-label FDA decision later this year, and the broad XLV ETF is the only demographics exposure in the current watchlist.

news_evidence.summary: Lilly Q1 2023 earnings: Mounjaro sales nearly $570M; SURMOUNT-1 data plus rolling submission to FDA seeking obesity indication in the coming weeks.
SURMOUNT-2 trial: >80% of patients on Mounjaro lost at least 5% body weight; results approaching SURMOUNT-1's ~21% weight-loss findings.

adds.rationale: NuScale Power is the only publicly-traded pure-play SMR developer; its 50 MWe design received final NRC certification in January 2023 and the company has since announced an MOU with Nucor for steel-mill baseload and a $275M public-private commitment for a Romania deployment, giving the watchlist a fission-SMR pure-play that URA/CCJ/BWXT/CEG do not provide.

news_evidence.summary: NRC certifies NuScale's 50 MWe SMR design; rule published Jan 19, 2023, effective Feb 21, 2023 — first SMR design certified in the US.
NuScale signs MOU with Nucor to explore deploying VOYGR SMRs to power Nucor's electric-arc-furnace steel mills.
Biden administration and partners commit up to $275M to advance a NuScale VOYGR SMR plant in Romania; RoPower Nuclear named as first international customer.

removes.rationale: URA (Global X Uranium ETF) overlaps heavily with CCJ — Cameco is its top holding — so dropping URA in favor of pure-play CCJ + the new SMR developer position gives the nuclear bucket cleaner, less correlated exposures without reducing the bucket's size.

news_evidence.summary: URA's portfolio is dominated by Cameco (CCJ) and a handful of uranium miners; holding both URA and CCJ duplicates the uranium-miner exposure already in the watchlist.

removes.rationale: Cognex's Q1 2023 results (May 4) flagged a step-down in factory-automation demand with e-commerce logistics customers pausing capex and consumer-electronics/semicap customers weakening — the fundamental robotics thesis is breaking, and BOTZ + ISRG continue to give the watchlist diversified robotics exposure.

news_evidence.summary: Cognex Q1 2023 earnings: factory-automation customers cautious with capital investment; significant step-down from record Q1 2022.
Several of Cognex's largest e-commerce customers paused most of their investments; broader factory-automation slowdown noted heading into 2023.

rationale_overall: Two thematic adjustments at quarter-end without changing watchlist size (24/24): rotate within nuclear from the URA basket (which is mostly Cameco) into pure-play SMR developer NuScale to capture the post-NRC-certification commercialization phase, and add Eli Lilly as a pure-play GLP-1/obesity demographics exposure alongside the broad XLV healthcare ETF. Funded by removing CGNX, whose Q1 2023 print confirmed an industrial-automation downturn, leaving BOTZ and ISRG to carry the robotics thesis. Net effect: nuclear stays at 4 with sharper sub-bucket diversity (uranium miner, naval/nuclear components, nuclear utility, SMR developer); demographics doubles from 1 to 2 in line with the profile's named aging-population wave; robotics trims one weakening single-name.
